name: Mirror of Aphrodite
description: The Mirror of Aphrodite is a legendary artifact said to have been crafted by Hephaestus himself as a gift for the goddess of love and beauty. It is not merely a reflective surface, but a conduit for Aphrodite's divine power, capable of amplifying beauty, revealing hidden truths about the heart, and even manipulating emotions related to love and desire. Legend states that the mirror's surface is made from polished celestial bronze and imbued with starlight, granting it an ethereal glow and unparalleled clarity. It is said that gazing into the mirror allows one to perceive not only their own physical appearance but also the true nature of their soul, their deepest desires, and their capacity for love. However, the mirror's power is not without its dangers. Some tales whisper that those who gaze too long into its depths can become consumed by vanity, driven mad by unrequited love, or even lose their sense of self. The mirror is more than just a reflection; it is a powerful tool that can both enhance and corrupt, depending on the intentions of the user and the strength of their heart. Its significance lies in its ability to explore the complexities of beauty, love, and the human condition, serving as a reminder that true beauty lies within and that love is a force that should be wielded with care and responsibility. The mirror remains a symbol of Aphrodite's influence on mortal affairs, a constant reminder of the power of beauty and the allure of love.
subtitle: Reflecting Beauty, Revealing Souls.
origin: Forged by Hephaestus as a gift for Aphrodite.
currentLocation: Unknown; rumored to be hidden on Mount Olympus or in a secret grotto.

keyMyths.title: The Judgment of Paris
keyMyths.description: While not directly involved, the Mirror of Aphrodite is symbolic of the beauty contest she won, influencing Paris's judgment.

keyMyths.title: Echo and Narcissus
keyMyths.description: Some versions suggest Narcissus's self-obsession was influenced by a similar enchanted pool/mirror reflecting only his beauty.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Describes Aphrodite's birth and divine attributes.
sources.text: Describes Aphrodite's birth and divine attributes.
